title: quercus ichnusae
intro: Quercus ichnusae, also known as the Ichnusa oak, is a species of evergreen oak tree native to Sardinia and Corsica. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Quercus ichnusae include its ability to protect against oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antiviral and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus ichnusae include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
